message:
feat(distributed): validate distributed agent IDs against wire-protocol sender rules

Distributed agent IDs are carried on every wire envelope and used as the
aggregator's session key, so they must satisfy the same grammar as
inbound `WireEnvelope.sender` fields. Three layers of the stack were
relying on free-form strings here, which let invalid identities slip
past config validation, leak into long-lived streams, and only blow up
once the aggregator rejected the first envelope.

Changes:

* `frankenterm-core::wire_protocol`
  - Promote `validate_sender_identity_with_limits` to `pub` and add a
    `validate_sender_identity` convenience wrapper that resolves against
    `WireProtocolLimits::default()`.
  - Document both as the canonical sender-identity gate so downstream
    callers stop reimplementing the rule.
  - Add a unit test asserting the public surface enforces non-empty,
    no-separator, length-bounded senders (including a tuned
    `max_sender_id_len = 5` regression case).

* `frankenterm-core::config::DistributedConfig`
  - `validate` now takes resolved `WireProtocolLimits` so it can apply
    the exact same gate the runtime uses.
  - `allow_agent_ids` entries are now (a) trimmed, (b) validated through
    `validate_sender_identity_with_limits`, and (c) deduplicated after
    ASCII-lowercase normalization. Previously a single empty-string
    check was the only guard, which permitted `agent:bad`, overlong IDs,
    and `Agent-1`/`agent-1` collisions.
  - Three new tests cover invalid characters, tuned overlong IDs, and
    duplicate-after-normalization rejection. Existing distributed tests
    were updated to use `token_env` (which is what the validator now
    requires) instead of the legacy bare `token` field.

* `frankenterm::main::run_distributed_agent`
  - Resolve and validate the agent ID *before* opening storage or
    starting the observation runtime, so a bad identity fails fast
    instead of after acquiring locks and warming caches.
  - Extract two helpers — `distributed_agent_resolve_id` (trim + fall
    back to default) and `distributed_agent_validate_id` (anyhow-wrapped
    `validate_sender_identity_with_limits`) — to keep the resolve step
    testable.
  - Add a `#[cfg(feature = "distributed")]` test pinning the helper to
    the wire rule, including a tuned-limit regression.
